text.title: Multi-Touch Attribution Modeling Failure Modes
text.summary: Antipatterns observed in real deployments of Multi-Touch Attribution Modeling. Each pairs symptom + sourced root-cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: Meta reports 500 conversions, Google reports 400, warehouse shows 600 unique; team budgets on platform sums.
antipattern.root-cause: warehouse-source-of-truth rule violated; platform sums double-count.
antipattern.fix: Set warehouse as truth; treat platforms as inputs; rebuild reconciliation.

antipattern.symptom: Budget shifted to bottom-funnel; organic ToFu starves; pipeline drops 40% in Q2.
antipattern.root-cause: four-model-variants rule violated; last-click ignored ToFu contribution.
antipattern.fix: Compare first-touch + position-based + time-decay; rebalance budget.

antipattern.symptom: Direct/none traffic is 35% of pipeline; team assumes 'brand'; cancels community budget; pipeline collapses.
antipattern.root-cause: dark-social-tracking rule violated; no investigation of direct/none source.
antipattern.fix: Add UTM landing-page surveys + first-call source question; attribute dark-social to actual channel.
